text:
This paper proposes a robust and accurate method for text detection from natural scene images. The basic character candidates, namely Maximally Stable Extremal Regions (MSERs), are extracted and a fast pruning method based on the strategy of minimizing regularized variations is adopted to eliminate most false detections caused by MSERs. The remaining candidates are linked into text lines using single-link clustering. A self-training distance metric learning algorithm is proposed to automatically decide the optimal distance weights and thresholds. Based on the clustering results and distances, the text candidates are discriminated from non-text ones using the posterior probabilities obtained from a designed character classifier. The candidates with higher non-text probabilities are eliminated and the remaining regions are verified by a text classifier.

In this paper, we propose a system designed to exploit the ICDAR 2011 Robust Reading Competition dataset. Experimental evaluation of the system demonstrates the effectiveness of proposed solution, achieving an f-measure of 76%, far beyond the existing 71% state of the art. In addition, a thorough experimental evaluation of the proposed solution against public multilingual dataset of natural scene text shows that an increase of 9% of f-measure above other competitive solutions in the literature. The metric learned, the associated classifier, and the identified structure in the features are the results of the integration of two components - automated metric learning and hierarchical pruning which improve significantly the robustness of the system to different font styles, to varying scale and to diverse complex background that appears in images of natural scene text.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
